tasks.title: Extend `plugin/src/temporal/worker-lock.ts` to v2 schema. Widen `WorkerLockContents.schema_version` union to `1 | 2`. Add optional `last_heartbeat: string` (ISO timestamp) and `expected_queue?: string` to v2 contents. Add `tryReclaimStaleLock(projectStateDir, options)` helper: on `acquireWorkerLock` failure, read existing lock; if PID dead (`process.kill(pid, 0)` ESRCH) → release + retry once; if v2 lock with `last_heartbeat` older than `STALE_HEARTBEAT_GRACE_MS` (default 60s) → release + retry once. Read of v1 contents must remain backward-compatible. Inline TDD: tests for v2 schema write/read, dead-PID reclaim, stale-heartbeat reclaim per rq-workerSingleton01.3/.5.

metadata.rationale: Lock primitive logic with reclaim branches

tasks.verification: Completed. Red/green TDD complete. Verification passed.
tasks.summary: Extended worker-lock contents to v1/v2 union, added v2 heartbeat and expected_queue write/read support, added configurable stale-heartbeat grace, dead-PID detection, and tryReclaimStaleLock wrapper that reclaims dead/stale holders and retries once while preserving live fresh locks.
tasks.implementation_summary: Extended worker-lock contents to v1/v2 union, added v2 heartbeat and expected_queue write/read support, added configurable stale-heartbeat grace, dead-PID detection, and tryReclaimStaleLock wrapper that reclaims dead/stale holders and retries once while preserving live fresh locks.

tasks.title: Add `plugin/src/temporal/worker-heartbeat.ts`. Implement heartbeat lifecycle for v2 worker locks: start a 10s unref'd timer, atomically rewrite `last_heartbeat`, stop renewing when local worker remains unserviceable past `SERVICEABILITY_GRACE_MS`, and release/cleanup on shutdown path. Inline TDD: tests for heartbeat update cadence using fake timers, atomic rewrite preserving lock owner fields, unref behavior, and self-expiry stop-renewing behavior per rq-workerSingleton01.5/.8.
tasks.verification: Completed. Red/green TDD complete. Verification passed after retry 3.
tasks.summary: Added worker-heartbeat lifecycle module for v2 locks. It starts an unref'd 10s heartbeat timer, atomically rewrites last_heartbeat while preserving lock owner fields, stops renewing after serviceability grace expires, and releases lock on explicit stop/shutdown path. Added tests for cadence, atomic rewrite, unref, and self-expiry.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added worker-heartbeat lifecycle module for v2 locks. It starts an unref'd 10s heartbeat timer, atomically rewrites last_heartbeat while preserving lock owner fields, stops renewing after serviceability grace expires, and releases lock on explicit stop/shutdown path. Added tests for cadence, atomic rewrite, unref, and self-expiry.

tasks.title: Add cross-cutting stability verification for AC-1 through AC-4. Create integration tests/harness that simulates three concurrent plugin init hosts for one project-id and asserts exactly one worker host, client-only peers, crash/dead-PID reclaim, stale-heartbeat reclaim, worktree guard BLOCK/PASS matrix, and status probe p95/coalescing under concurrent calls. Run targeted tests plus `pnpm test` from `plugin/`. Mark as `separate_verification` intent during execution because it verifies multiple implemented components together.
tasks.verification: Harden remediation verified: targeted tests passed (11 tests); pnpm run check passed; pnpm test passed; pnpm run build passed; Bun lru-cache import/cache smoke passed

wisdom.content: `adv_task_update` rejects `status: "cancelled"` before any signal mutation. Worktree isolation guard still needs to run before that rejection when guarding all main-checkout status-transition attempts, otherwise enabled guard would not cover direct cancelled attempts. Hook order: resolve task/change → evaluate guard → existing cancellation-policy rejection → Temporal signal path.

For worktree detection regressions, a small real-git temp fixture gives stronger coverage than mocked `rev-parse`: create main checkout + linked worktree, then assert main=false, linked=true, non-git=false against the shared git-session helper.

`lru-cache` with `allowStale: true` returns stale entries immediately on TTL expiry. For probe cache semantics where ordinary TTL expiry should refresh but abort/rejection should fall back to stale, pass `allowStale: false` to `cache.fetch()` while keeping `allowStaleOnFetchAbort/Rejection` and `ignoreFetchAbort` enabled.

Safety-critical restart verification can use probe-cache for bounded probes, but success must require non-stale freshness. Use forceRefresh for verification loops and treat stale cached serviceability as diagnostic-only, not proof for restart/reclaim success.

Full `pnpm run format` can reformat earlier task files during a later verification task. If Prettier drift appears late, rerun targeted docs/assets, `pnpm run check`, full `pnpm test`, and `pnpm run build` before checkpoint so the final verification commit captures all style-only normalization safely.

proposal.approval_evidence: Origin: roadmap → #118 (critical). Linked: #117, #107. Ambiguity scan B:C F:C S:C M:C, zero blocking findings. Research artifacts (3 LBP reports) carry forward to discovery.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery converged on proposal direction with no contract compromise. Q:P ambiguity (p95 baseline) deferred to first apply task as instrumented baseline collection — not blocking.

design.approval_evidence: Design conforms to existing rq-workerSingleton01 (.1-.8) without spec mutation. New requirements rq-worktreeMutationGuard01 (worktree-lifecycle) and rq-statusProbeCache01 (advance-meta) drafted. New scenario rq-workerSingleton01.9 (worker_role visibility) drafted. Latent v2 suspect classification bug fix folded in. Implementation order published for prep gate task graph.
